title: euryops polytrichoides
intro: Euryops polytrichoides, also known as the everlast plant or Camphor bush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern Cape region of South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, fever, and wounds, and are also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits like relieving colds, coughs, and skin irritations. Euryops polytrichoides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications, such as creams and salves.
